vendor: NexusDB, ElevateDB, Apollo, DBISAM, Advantage, Absolute Database, IBObjects
description: Conservative preservation/review pack for common Delphi database engines. Nonvisual dataset, query, session, connection, and table components may be preserved only when compatible packages and target-platform support exist; legacy engines and data-aware UI controls require manual review.

rules.notes: NexusDB session is nonvisual and may be preserved with compatible NexusDB packages.
rules.manual_review_reason: Verify package support, embedded/remote engine setup, aliases, and target platform.

rules.notes: NexusDB database component is nonvisual and may be preserved.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review aliases, paths, transactions, and deployment.

rules.notes: NexusDB table component may be preserved.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review table paths, indexes, field definitions, and binding strategy.

rules.notes: NexusDB query component may be preserved.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review SQL dialect, parameters, updates, and connection references.
Embedded server engine lifecycle, threading, and deployment require review.

rules.notes: ElevateDB session is nonvisual and may be preserved with compatible packages.
rules.manual_review_reason: Verify ElevateDB edition/platform support and deployment libraries.

rules.notes: ElevateDB database component is nonvisual and may be preserved.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review database paths, configuration, and platform storage.
Review table metadata, indexes, and binding.
Review SQL, parameters, transactions, and update behavior.
Apollo VCL data components wrap a legacy data engine and require package/platform review before preservation.
Apollo table migration should be reviewed with the database-engine modernization plan.
Apollo query migration requires SQL, connection, and package review.
Absolute Database preservation depends on package/platform support and deployment needs.
Review table paths, encryption, indexes, and binding.
Review SQL dialect, parameters, and update behavior.
DBISAM migration should be reviewed for engine version, platform support, and possible ElevateDB/FireDAC modernization.
DBISAM table migration requires database-engine review.
DBISAM SQL migration requires dialect and package review.
Advantage Database Server is legacy/Windows-oriented in many projects and requires explicit migration planning.
Advantage table migration requires database-engine review.
Advantage SQL migration requires database-engine review.
IBObjects preservation depends on package support, transaction handling, and target-platform deployment.
Review transaction scope and connection references.
Review SQL, parameters, live queries, and dataset binding.
TDataSet-compatible IBObjects query may be preservable but requires binding and package review.
Review table metadata, indexes, and binding.
IBObjects visual/data-aware controls require manual FMX UI and binding redesign.
